title: Artifact stream observability centers repository artifacts
text: MSO graph observability now treats workflow supply-chain nodes as Artifacts rather than generic Data nodes. data_type remains the storage/access medium, while artifact_type expresses operational meaning: knowledge_store, event_store, local_database, document, or media. This matches the repository execution model where tasks read repository artifacts, parse/query/reason over their internal data, form knowledge, and serialize new artifacts. The canonical outputs are artifact-stream-report.md and artifact-stream-views/, while resource-stream-* and data-stream-* remain deprecated v0.4.0 compatibility aliases.
author: agent

metadata.rationale: Workflow topology review is about an Artifact Supply Chain, not a raw data pipeline. Rendering PRD/report/prompt/image files, ontology TTL, work-memory logs, local databases, and media deliverables as the same Data node obscured consumer intent, terminal deliverables, and missing agent consumers.
alternatives: Keep resource_kind=file|data as the canonical model; rejected because it still centers the file/data dichotomy rather than the repository artifact lifecycle.
Add workflow TBox predicate wf:artifactType immediately; deferred because this patch intentionally updates observability/reporting and index schema first without expanding the workflow TTL contract.
Classify everything by file extension only; rejected because roles such as ontology, workflow, work-memory, auditlog, and deliverable carry stronger operational meaning than extension alone.
